libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests that call ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(...) without the overload that accepts allowedFactoryClassNames, set the system property `com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist` to the fully qualified test ObjectFactory class name(s), comma-separated, before the call and clear/restore it afterward. Prefer a narrow whitelist for the specific test factory class. Alternatively, use the ReferenceableUtils overload that accepts an explicit Set of allowed factory class names.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #2097 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.4.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The whitelist is a JVM-global system property, so tests should restore the previous value to avoid order-dependent behavior.

The artifact has optional integrations for Typesafe Config/HOCON and logging backends, but they are not required before meaningful coverage is reachable; tests that deliberately import those adapter APIs may need explicit optional dependencies.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: mchange-commons-java 0.4.0 adds a security requirement for ReferenceableUtils: resolving a javax.naming.Reference through overloads without an explicit allowed-factory Set now requires an ObjectFactory whitelist configuration.
